name: Cornish Pasty
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the pastry dough and chill for about an hour before using.
2. Finely chop the meat.
3. Mix the chopped vegetables with the meat, herbs, and season well.
4. Roll out the pastry to a large round.
5. Place the meat and vegetable mixture in the center of the pastry.
6. Fold over one side of the pastry to form a half round.
7. Crimp the edges and glaze with milk.
8. Bake in a moderately hot oven for 1 hour. Note that the filling should always be raw.
9. The Cornish pasty can be made as one large one or several small ones. In Cornwall, they were eaten by the tin and copper miners at "Crib" time.
(Note: This recipe is from Celtic Cookbook by Helen SMITH-TWIDDY, published in 1979.)
